narrative: Trump’s weekend threat of 25% tariffs on European autos and stalled US-Iran peace talks dominate the tape, setting up a defensive posture for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; the tariff escalation and a +175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage likely to cap upside. JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + geopolitical risk premium: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7), USD 1W avg: -0.879% (n=7), G8 spread: 1.960%. This divergence, fueled by confirmed $34.5bn yen intervention and geopolitical friction, would pressure USD/JPY from its Friday close of 157.03. RBA’s Tuesday meeting remains the primary TIER-1 focus, where a hawkish hold is likely to support AUD/USD (Friday close: 0.7208) despite the broader risk-off tone.
